title: Data Quality with Great Expectations Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of data validation, expectation suites, checkpoints, and pipeline integration.

questions.question: In Great Expectations, what is the relationship between an Expectation Suite and a Checkpoint?

options.text: They are synonyms — both describe a set of validation rules
An Expectation Suite contains the validation rules; a Checkpoint configures how and when to run those rules against a batch of data and what to do with the results
A Checkpoint contains expectations; a Suite runs them on a schedule
Expectation Suites are for pandas; Checkpoints are for Spark

questions.question: Why should data quality validations run BEFORE transformations in a pipeline, not after?

options.text: Transformations cannot run if validation hasn't been registered first
Running complex transformations on bad data wastes compute resources and pollutes downstream tables — it's far cheaper to fail fast at the source
Post-transform validation is always inaccurate because transformations change the data schema
Pre-transform validation catches performance bottlenecks before they reach the transformation step
Tables growing too large and consuming excessive storage
Empty or near-empty dataset bugs where a pipeline bug caused no records to be ingested — common with broken source connections or API failures
Duplicate rows that cause double-counting in downstream reports
Slow query performance on tables with fewer than 1000 rows

questions.question: How should Expectation Suites be stored and version-controlled?

options.text: In the Great Expectations Data Docs web UI only
In git alongside the pipeline code — data quality expectations are as fundamental as unit tests and should be reviewed and versioned like code
In a database table separate from the codebase for easier runtime access
In S3 as JSON files managed by a dedicated team outside engineering

questions.question: In the Airflow integration, what does `fail_task_on_validation_failure=True` in the GreatExpectationsOperator do?

options.text: Retries the Airflow task automatically when validation fails
Sends an email to the data owner but allows the DAG to continue
Marks the Airflow task as FAILED when any expectation fails, stopping downstream tasks from running on bad data
Logs a warning and continues but skips the transformation step
